title: LATTICE: Evaluating Decision Support Utility of Crypto Agents

text.extraction_method: pdftotext -layout; UTF-8 text with page breaks preserved

official_repository.paper_time_relation: This latest pre-v1 commit predates immutable arXiv v1 submission by 8 days. It is the timing-appropriate pinned release for paper correspondence auditing, not proof that the archived files were the exact private experiment state.
official_repository.acquisition_head_relation: HEAD is approximately 23 hours after v1 submission. Its sole change from the pinned pre-v1 archive is README.md, adding the arXiv link and citation; all other 27 regular files are byte-identical.
official_repository.archive_integrity: Both ZIP archives passed CRC verification. Each contains 42 members (28 regular files), including 1,200 queries, six 1,200-response files, scores, rubric, judge prompt, query-generation assets, and scoring code.

verification.pdfinfo_title: LATTICE: Evaluating Decision Support Utility of Crypto Agents
verification.text_inspection: Non-empty extraction inspected from title and abstract through methods and results to the human-preference study, limitations, references, and final page boundary. All 15 page breaks are present and no UTF-8 replacement characters were found.
verification.source_archive_integrity: Archive listed successfully; 15 members include section TeX, main.tex, bibliography, style, chart sources, and the benchmark figure.
verification.repository_archive_inspection: Both complete archives passed CRC checks. The pre-v1 release exposes 1,200 final queries, six complete response files, scores_1200.json, the six-dimension rubric, judge instructions, query provenance and generation code, scoring code, requirements, and smoke CI. Representative JSON assets parsed successfully; filtered_queries_1200.json contains 1,200 records.

review_boundary: This record establishes immutable-paper acquisition, successful full-text extraction, and timing-aware preservation of the official release. It is not a deep review and does not establish score reproduction, rubric validity, judge calibration or robustness, human-preference validity, observed decision improvement, financial benefit, safety, professional validity, production fitness, or readiness.
